questions.q: What is the main difference between the built-in function and user-defined function in Python?
questions.answer: The built-in function is a pre-defined function in Python's library, whereas a user-defined function is a function created by the user.

questions.q: Write a Python function to calculate the area of a circle given its radius.
Which of the following is a valid use of the lambda function in Python?
questions.answer: d) To create small, one-time use functions

questions.q: What is the purpose of the return statement in a function?
questions.answer: The return statement is used to exit a function and pass a value back to the caller.

questions.q: Write a Python function to find the maximum of three numbers.
Which of the following is a feature of a lambda function in Python?
questions.answer: a) It can take multiple arguments

questions.q: What is the difference between a function and a method in Python?
questions.answer: A function is a block of code that can be called from anywhere in the program, whereas a method is a block of code that belongs to a class and is called on an object.

questions.q: Write a Python function to print the first 10 natural numbers.
Which of the following is a valid argument for a function in Python?
What is the purpose of the pass statement in a function?
questions.answer: The pass statement is used as a placeholder when a statement is required syntactically but no execution of code is necessary.
